sae_features.label: VWA/I-domain MIDAS adhesion surface
sae_features.description: von Willebrand factor A (VWA)/integrin I-like A-domain core and homologous A-domain–like subdomains, specifically the acidic/polar helical/loop surface (MIDAS or pseudo-MIDAS) used for metal-dependent or analogous adhesion/interaction

sae_features.label: VWA/I-like MIDAS interfaces
sae_features.description: VWA/integrin I-like adhesion module and closely mimicked acidic/polar interface patches used for divalent-cation–mediated or partner-binding interactions in both extracellular and intracellular assembly factors.

sae_features.label: Acidic S/T-rich IDRs
sae_features.description: Low-complexity, intrinsically disordered segments enriched in Ser/Thr together with acidic residues (often also Gly/Pro) that serve as flexible stalks/linkers, propeptides, or regulatory tails—extracellularly functioning as mucin-like, O-glycosylated regions in secreted/cell-surface adhesion proteins, and cytosolically as acidic, phosphorylation-prone tails in trafficking/adhesion machinery

sae_features.label: Extracellular β-rich adhesion modules
sae_features.description: Conserved β‑strand/short‑helix segments that make up β‑rich extracellular adhesion/recognition modules—most prominently the integrin α FG‑GAP β‑propeller blades and tandem SRCR domains—with weaker, sporadic matches at analogous strand↔helix junctions within diverse non‑extracellular folds

sae_features.label: Beta sandwich and propeller core
sae_features.description: A structural signal for β-rich interaction modules in eukaryotic proteins—principally β-sandwich domains (C2, YEATS, CBM21) and also some β-propellers (e.g., WD40)—with occasional extension into adjacent acidic, Ser/Thr/Pro-rich low-complexity tails; these modules are common in trafficking/adaptor, chromatin-reader, and regulatory proteins and the feature marks the β-strand core rather than catalytic domains.

sae_features.label: Beta-propeller blade-end loops
sae_features.description: Recognition of beta-propeller blade termini: the C-terminal strand-to-loop junctions that cap each repeat blade in diverse beta-propeller domains (WD40, FG-GAP/integrin-alpha, Kelch, Sema, and enzymatic propellers), typically enriched for Gly/Asp/Trp (e.g., WD motif) and forming ligand-, ion-, or substrate-facing loops; common in extracellular/periplasmic scaffolds and enzymes.

sae_features.label: Charged junctional interaction loops
sae_features.description: Short, charged loop segments at secondary-structure junctions (often β‑strand–α‑helix, but also coil–helix or β‑turns) that act as interaction or divalent‑metal coordination hotspots. The canonical case is the MIDAS loop of von Willebrand A/I domains, but analogous loops occur in diverse α/β folds (AAA+/FtsK-, tubulin-like NTPases, coat/adaptor proteins such as Sec23/24). In extracellular proteins these loops frequently coordinate Ca2+/Mg2+ for adhesion; in ubiquitination modules they can be Zn‑chelating Cys/His cross‑braces (RING) or adjacent acidic loops mediating E2/E3 interactions.

sae_features.label: Acidic VWA-like adhesion surfaces
sae_features.description: Exposed acidic/Ser/Thr-rich ligand/metal-binding surfaces, most commonly within Von Willebrand factor type A (VWA/A-domain) and closely related adhesin modules (e.g., VWA-like regions in PilY1 and the edge of PA14 domains), but also recurring on non-VWA scaffolds with analogous surface chemistry used for macromolecular binding (e.g., COPII cargo adaptor Sec24 and the Ku70/80 DNA-end–binding complex)
